1. Expressed delight, I encountered Toptal and worked with an expert within a few several hours.

13. Concluded, I emphasized the significance of careful planning in app development.

1. Completed actions, BISSNEX completed key market expansion actions and gained recognition from the U.S. federal government and financial systems.  
2. Acquired license, BISSNEX acquired the U.S. MSB license, signaling strengthening of the platform's capabilities.  
3. Showed friendliness, the U.S. showed rising friendliness towards the crypto industry, supporting cryptocurrency taxation as property.  
4. Remained cautious, the U.S. maintained a cautious attitude towards cryptocurrencies as the industry evolved.  
5. Implemented standards, governments worldwide gradually implemented trading and regulatory standards for the crypto industry.  
6. Circulated cryptocurrencies, the widespread circulation of mainstream cryptocurrencies made BISSNEX attentive to expanding into the U.S. market.  
7. Released report, The International Organization of Securities Commissions released the Consultation Report on Policy Recommendations for Crypto-Asset.  
8. Obtained license, BISSNEX obtained the Money Services Business license from the U.S. Financial Crimes Enforcement Network.  
9. Demonstrated compliance, BISSNEX demonstrated compliance with the highest industry standards in Anti-Money Laundering and Know Your Customer practices.  
10. Expanded services, BISSNEX planned to expand its services with higher compliance standards.

1. Showed performance, indicators indicated sustainable forest management in 2023 exceeded the target, said Agus Justianto, acting director general of sustainable forest management at the Ministry of Environment and Forestry on Thursday.  
2. Helped make management inclusive, accelerated implementation of the multi-forestry business model in forest utilization business licensing (PBPH) involved the community.  
3. Informed planting and enrichment, Justianto reported that 667,780 hectares of the PBPH area underwent planting and enrichment in 2023.  
4. Exceeded target, the figure of 667,780 hectares was 156 percent above the target of 428 thousand hectares.  
5. Disclosed log production, Justianto stated that log production reached 58.13 million cubic meters, or 101.9 percent of the target of 57 million cubic meters.  
6. Reported sources of log production, Justianto noted that 89.01 percent came from plantation forests, 7.37 percent from natural forests, 1.59 percent from BUMN Perhutani, and 2.03 percent from non-forestry.  
7. Used logs, the forest products processing industry utilized logs as raw materials to create various products such as woodworking products, plywood, wood chips, wood panels, and pulp.  
8. Increased production, Justianto mentioned that production of non-timber forest products like rattan, agarwood, latex, and bamboo reached 820.2 thousand tons in 2023.

1. Disclosed agreement, Embark Technology, Inc. and Applied Intuition, Inc. entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger on May 25, 2023.  
2. Merged entities, Azara Merger Sub, Inc. merged with and into Embark, with Embark continuing as a wholly owned subsidiary of Applied.  
3. Filed complaint, Marceil A. Hanshew filed a putative class action complaint in the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware on June 26, 2023.  
4. Named defendants, Complaint named Embark and the then-members of Embark's Board of Directors as Defendants in A. Hanshew v. Embark Technology, Inc. et al.  
5. Alleged false statements, Complaint alleged that disclosures in the preliminary proxy statement contained false and misleading statements.  
6. Sought relief, Complaint sought a preliminary injunction to enjoin the Merger.  
7. Filed proxy statement, Embark filed a definitive proxy statement on Schedule 14A with the SEC on June 26, 2023.  
8. Amended complaint, Plaintiffs filed an amended complaint captioned David L. Hanshew in His Capacity as Attorney-in-Fact for Marceil A. Hanshew and Marceil A. Hanshew on June 29, 2023.

1. Announced highlights, Centre for Ecocities shared work highlights for 2023 and plans for 2024.  
2. Kicked off projects, Centre initiated new projects over the past few months.  
3. Launched International Ecological Footprint Learning Lab, Centre collaborated with York University and the University of Iceland, funded by SSHRC.  
4. Improved methodologies, project aimed to enhance knowledge and student training in ecological footprinting at the urban scale.  
5. Developed Urban Consumption Guidebook for Cities, Centre worked with C40 Cities, OneEarth Living, and Hot or Cool Institute.  
6. Planned guide launch, Centre scheduled interactive resources for spring release.  
7. Created BC-specific Policy and Planning Guide, Centre received funding from the Real Estate Foundation of BC.  
8. Addressed priorities, guide aimed to tackle issues indicated by Consumption-Based Emission Inventories (CBEIs) while advancing equity.  
9. Developed archetype tool, Centre simplified creation of CBEIs and Ecological Footprint assessments for communities across British Columbia.  
10. Completed inventories, Centre used ecoCity Footprint Tool developed by Dr. Jennie Moore for Ecological Footprint assessments.  
11. Engaged Peer Network, Christina Olsen coordinated efforts for sustainability and climate action in Victoria.  
12. Led initiatives, Christina focused on enhancing ecological sustainability across British Columbia.

1. Noted time of year, families spent days together at home and started challenging new jigsaw puzzles.  
2. Identified problem, many families failed to have the right-sized surface for all puzzle pieces.  
3. Solved problem, research paper revealed best table size was just under twice the size of the assembled puzzle.  
4. Completed jigsaws, Dr. Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher and Dr. Kent Bonsma-Fisher completed nine jigsaws with pieces ranging from nine to 2,000.  
5. Measured area, researchers found unassembled jigsaw puzzle took up an area about 1.7 times its finished size.  
6. Laid out pieces, researchers placed pieces naturally for each puzzle without artificial proximity.  
7. Completed jigsaws, researchers painstakingly finished each jigsaw and calculated length and width of the assembled picture.  
8. Discovered theory, researchers found a simple theory predicted area needed for pieces regardless of number.  
9. Expressed surprise, Dr. Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher of the University of Toronto gasped at the discovery.  
10. Reported results, findings showed incredible agreement between theory and practice, as noted in New Scientist.

2. Removed credit card debt, participants removed their credit card debt and raised their savings.  
3. Made prudent investments, users made prudent investments and experienced financial freedom.  
4. Recommended daily use, experts recommended utilizing the Billionaire Brain Wave daily for a full thirty day period.

6. Enhanced waves, researchers utilized sound waves to enhance Theta waves for manifesting success.  
7. Listened regularly, individuals listened to the Billionaire Brain Wave system regularly to reach their goals.  
8. Concluded value, analysts concluded that The Billionaire Brain Wave was an excellent purchase for an affordable price.  
9. Created program, Billionaire Brain Wave was created by Dave Mitchell, an average individual facing difficulties.

1. Rediscovered plants, community scientists rediscovered plants hidden deep within the tropical Andes after more than 100 years.  
2. Included plants, rediscovered plants included Nasa genus from the Blazing Star family (Loasaceae).  
3. Perplexed scientists, scientists were perplexed by plants due to their delicate yet painfully stinging leaves.  
4. Found plants, most of these plants were found in specific regions and were rare.  
5. Depended scientists, scientists depended on herbaria for plant data in the past.  
6. Accessed data, scientists accessed biodiversity data through global networking and freely accessible data repositories.  
7. Utilized platform, iNaturalist was utilized by users to post photographic records of plant occurrences.  
8. Instrumental platform, iNaturalist proved instrumental in the rediscovery of Andean plants.  
9. Documented species, Nasa colanii species had only been documented once in 1978.  
10. Stumbled upon photograph, research team stumbled upon a 2019 photograph of Nasa colanii.  
11. Identified species, Nasa ferox was identified in 2022 when users uploaded its photographs on iNaturalist.  
12. Unreported duration, Nasa ferox had gone unreported for around 130 years.  
13. Located sanctuary, Nasa colanii was native to an isolated cloud forest in Peru’s Cordillera de Colán National Sanctuary.  
14. Situated elevation, Cordillera de Colán National Sanctuary was situated at an elevation of 2,605 meters above sea level.  
15. Mentioned location, the event occurred in LIMA, Peru.

1. Announced progress, Katherine Bennell-Pegg announced she was halfway through her year-long training at the European Astronaut Centre in Germany.  
2. Relocated family, Ms Bennell-Beg and her family relocated to Europe for intensive training.  
3. Applied for position, more than 22,000 people applied to the coveted astronaut candidate position.  
4. Required training, the job required rigorous physical training and a year of intensive study.  
5. Expressed dream, Katherine Bennell-Pegg expressed she had always dreamed of being an astronaut.  
6. Explained motivation, Katherine Bennell-Pegg explained her motivation shifted from adventure to addressing real-world problems.  
7. Expected activities, the next six months were expected to include intense exercise, sea survival, and studying space station robotic arm simulations.  
8. Discussed medical training, Katherine Bennell-Pegg discussed the need to know medical procedures for astronauts.  
9. Highlighted research support, Katherine Bennell-Pegg highlighted how space conditions accelerated medical issues for research support.  
10. Served previously, Katherine Bennell-Pegg previously served as Director of Space at the Australian Space Agency.

1. Continued offering free games, Epic Games Store made certain games free every day during holiday sales.  
2. Announced free game, Epic Games announced Cat Quest as the free game on December 28.  
3. Released game, Cat Quest, dubbed “Diablo the Cat,” was released in 2017.  
4. Developed game, Gentlebros developed Cat Quest, featuring real-time combat and dungeon adventures.  
5. Set game, Cat Quest was set in the colorful world of Felingard.

7. Offered game, Epic Games made Cursed to Golf free before December 29.

10. Instructed steps, Epic Games provided steps to get the game for free.

12. Entered game page, users entered the “Cat Quest” page.  
13. Clicked purchase button, users clicked the purchase button on the right side.  
14. Waited for process, users waited for the process to complete.

1. Starred in documentary, Mark Kistler starred in The Secret Cities of Mark Kistler, which told the story of Kistler’s legacy.  
2. Featured Corgan Pictures, Corgan Pictures produced the documentary.  
3. Had big bright eyes, Commander Mark had big bright eyes and a wide mustachioed smile.  
4. Wore bright red coveralls, Commander Mark wore bright red military coveralls filled with magic markers.  
5. Used catchphrase, Commander Mark used the catchphrase “Dream it. Draw it. Do it.”  
6. Taught millions, Millions of children tuned in to PBS to watch Commander Mark teach drawing.  
7. Released documentary, Forty years later, a new documentary told the story of Mark Kistler.  
8. Created shows, Mark Kistler created the shows The Secret City, Draw Squad and Imagination Station.  
9. Born in Ohio, Mark Kistler was born in Ohio.  
10. Grew up in Southern California, Mark Kistler grew up in Southern California as the youngest of five Kistlers.  
11. Encouraged by mother, Kistler’s mother encouraged him to follow his passion for art.  
12. Bought art supplies, Kistler’s mother bought him art supplies for his artistic pursuits.  
13. Turned closet into studio, Kistler turned his bedroom closet into a tiny art studio.  
14. Loved Disney animations, Kistler loved Disney animations and dreamed of becoming an animator.  
15. Taught drawing workshops, Kistler began teaching drawing workshops for kids as a teenager.  
16. Fell in love with teaching, Kistler fell in love with teaching art through local parks and rec programming.  
17. Set goal, Kistler set a goal to teach a million kids to draw.  
18. Believed in idea, Kistler believed anyone could learn to draw.

1. Worked to build better bread, scientists worked in the farm fields near Mount Vernon.  
2. Said Dr. Stephen Jones, bread is science involving physics, chemistry, and biology.  
3. Explored science of tasty whole grain loaves, The Washington State University Bread Lab explored since 2011.

10. Said baking instructor Juli Hammond, classes aimed to reduce intimidation for students.

13. Mentioned measurement, recipe included 14 cup of ingredients for baking.  
14. Spent time baking, students spent about one hour in the Cooking with Whole Grains class.

1. Stated CEO RJ Scaringe, Rivian’s upcoming R2 model would be its version of the Tesla Model 3.  
2. Noted that Rivian’s R2 would help shape the EV startup’s future despite not resembling the Tesla Model 3.  
3. Mentioned that Tesla released the Roadster in 2008, leading to the Model S in 2012.  
4. Announced Tesla's first mass-market electric car, the Model 3, several years later.  
5. Described by CEO Elon Musk, Tesla went through “production hell” ramping up the Model 3.  
6. Became the best-selling EV globally, Tesla’s Model 3 held the title until the Model Y topped it in 2020.  
7. Predicted that Tesla’s Model 3 would be among the top-selling vehicles globally in 2023.  
8. Reported that Rivian ramped production all year despite rivals cutting EV targets.  
9. Raised production goal, Rivian aimed to build 52,000 vehicles this year following strong Q3 results.  
10. Produced more than double the 24,337 vehicles last year, Rivian increased its production target.  
11. Chose Clayco as its partner earlier this month to build its $5 billion EV plant in Georgia.  
12. Stated that the new facility would be home to Rivian’s R2 vehicles.  
13. Mentioned that Scaringe previously said R2 would start at around $40,000 to $50,000.

6. Gained importance, private space companies gained significance in the space industry during 2023.  
7. Planned mission, NASA planned to set foot on the Moon’s surface in 2024.  
8. Anticipated mission, Artemis II flight aimed to bring 4 astronauts within 80 miles of the Moon.  
9. Cooperated with SpaceX, NASA partnered with SpaceX for the Artemis III flight to land on the Moon.  
10. Developed rocket, SpaceX worked on the Starship Rocket for the Artemis missions.  
11. Performed tests, SpaceX conducted 2 tests of the Starship Rocket, which had not yet reached orbit.  
12. Planned launch, NASA scheduled the Artemis Mission for November 2024 to test the new Space Launch System (SLS) rocket.  
13. Followed path, astronauts planned to follow the same path as the uncrewed Artemis mission during the launch.
